rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
The authoritative source for component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections is the input AADL model
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the most specific valid scope for the requirement
Preserve the AADL architecture by maintaining the original component hierarchy and connections
Do not introduce new subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in implementation annexes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not introduce new subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
